description: Pilot: cursor-agent (composer-2.5) judging patch equivalence in read-only mode.

pairs.agent_reasoning: Both patches apply the same RFC 7231 two-digit-year sliding window in parse_http_date, differing only in whether the reference year is taken from utcnow() or now(). That is a real behavioral fork: the task's test patch mocks only utcnow, so patch A passes the fail-to-pass RFC850 cases while B does not, and the functions can disagree whenever the local timezone's year differs from the UTC year. Same fix intent and nearly identical code, but not observably equivalent on all inputs that matter for this bug.

Both patches fix the same bug by replacing max(powers.values()) with sum(powers.values()) for min-degree filtering in itermonomials' total-degree mode. Patch A also checks total_deg <= max_degree, but that is already enforced by generating tuples of length max_degree, so the yielded monomial sets match. The only other difference is A's added regression tests, which do not change library runtime behavior.

Patches A and B are byte-identical: each removes the single s.replace(".[", "[") call in getmodpath() and returns s unchanged. Only src/_pytest/python.py is modified, and the applied A/ and B/ trees match on that change. They therefore implement the same fix with no behavioral divergence.
